column1: Lisa was getting ready for bed. She'd just brushed her_______-, and was deciding which book to read  in bed. (TOOTH)
As usual, she was thinking about million other things at the same time. "Daddy's got the____________job in the whole world', she thought. (INTERESTING)
I wonder if I'll be a teacher like Daddy when I'm____________", she then thought. "Maybe I'll be an astronaut". (OLD)
She smiled to___________at the idea of speeding through space in a spaceship. (SHE)
Then she stopped___________. "But If I'm an astronaut, I'll be away from my family, friends and cat". (SMILE)
She__________it was impossible to be an astronaut and see her family all the time. (KNOW)
She___________a decision. "I won't do that job, then. I want to see my cat every evening." (MAKE)
At that moment, her cat Timothy_________into the room. Timothy looked at her, as if to say: "I want to see you every day too". (COME)
"Now" said Lisa. "What book do you want_________to read to you before bed tonight, Timothy?" (I)
Red Square is a city square in the centre of Moscow. It_____________by some people to be centre of all of Russia. (CONSIDER)
Its name______________from the red bricks that make up the square, nor from any connection to a type of government. (NOT COME)
Rather, the word 'red' in Russian can also mean 'beautiful'. As the square has grand and beautiful buildings,________appearance is indeed lovely. (IT)
Originally, the square____________to be a market-place, but it has also been used for other purposes. (DESIGN)
Official ceremonies_____________place there over the years, sush as the crowning of tsars in past centuries. (TAKE)
Around the square, there___________several important buildings, sush as the Kremlin and St Basil's Cathedral. (BE)
In 1990, Red Square___________a UNESCO World Heritage Site, along with the Kremlin. (BECOME)
Red Square is also used for rock concerts___________days. Performers sush as Paul Mc-Cartney, Shakira and the Red Hot Chili Peppers have played there. (THIS)
McCartney's band, The Beatles, was not allowed to play in Russia. McCartney, however, was the_________Beatle to perform in Russia, after Ringo Starr played there in 1998. (TWO)
